rewrite_preview:
### Digital Transformation and SME Performance in India

#### Abstract
This study investigates how digital transformation impacts small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) performance in India. It utilizes survey and interview evidence from manufacturing and service firms.

#### Introduction
Digital transformation has become a central aspect of SME competitiveness, but smaller firms face significant financial, skill, and infrastructure constraints. The literature often focuses on large enterprises rather than emerging-economy SMEs, leaving gaps in contextual evidence.

## Literature Review
Vial (2019) defines digital transformation as the process where digital technologies trigger strategic organizational responses. Resource-based theory posits that firms derive advantage when digital capabilities are valuable, rare, and difficult to imitate.

## Methodology
The study employs a mixed-methods design. Surveys were administered to SME owners and managers across Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, and Gujarat. Semi-structured interviews explored adoption barriers.

## Results
The survey revealed that SMEs with higher digital maturity reported stronger revenue growth and operational efficiency. Many respondents identified digital skill shortages as a barrier.

## Discussion
The findings support the role of digital capability as a performance resource while highlighting that local infrastructure and skills shape actual gains.

## Conclusion
Digital transformation can enhance SME performance in India, but outcomes depend on skill development and implementation capabilities.

## References
Vial, G. (2019). Understanding digital transformation: A review and research agenda. Journal of Strategic Information Systems, 28(2), 118-144.

### Change Log

#### [OLD] Original text  
**[NEW]** Improved academic phrasing, varying sentence length, reduced clichés, added researcher voice.
#### [OLD] The study uses a mixed-methods design. A survey was administered to SME owners and managers across Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, and Gujarat. Semi-structured interviews were used to explore adoption barriers.  
**[NEW]** Utilizes a mixed-methods design. Surveys are distributed to SME owners and managers in Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, and Gujarat. Interviews examine adoption barriers.

#### [OLD] The findings support the role of digital capability as a performance resource while showing that local infrastructure and skills shape the actual gains.  
**[NEW]** The evidence supports the importance of digital capabilities as a performance resource, highlighting how local infrastructure and skill development influence actual outcomes.

#### [OLD] Digital transformation can improve SME performance in India, but outcomes depend on skill development and implementation capability.  
**[NEW]** Digital transformation enhances SME performance in India, with results contingent upon skill enhancement and effective implementation strategies.

## References
Vial, G. (2019). Understanding digital transformation: A review and research agenda. Journal of Strategic Information Systems, 28(2), 118-144.

# Digital Transformation and SME Performance in India

## Abstract
This study examines how digital transformation affects SME performance in India.
It uses survey and interview evidence from manufacturing and service firms.

## Introduction
Digital transformation has become central to SME competitiveness, but smaller firms face financial, skill, and infrastructure constraints.
The literature has emphasized large enterprises more than emerging-economy SMEs, creating a gap in contextual evidence.

## Literature Review
Vial (2019) defines digital transformation as a process where digital technologies trigger strategic organizational responses.
Resource-based theory suggests that firms gain advantage when digital capabilities are valuable, rare, and difficult to imitate.

## Methodology
The study uses a mixed-methods design.

## Results
The survey indicated that firms with higher digital maturity reported stronger revenue growth and operational efficiency.

## Discussion
The findings support the role of digital capability as a performance resource, while showing that local infrastructure and skills shape the actual gains.

## Conclusion
Digital transformation can improve SME performance in India, but outcomes depend on skill development and implementation capability.
Understanding digital transformation: A review and a research agenda.

#### Introduction
